title: Energy, work and power
summary: 9709 P4 — work, energy, power, and conservation principles with PhET Energy Skate Park.
learningObjectives: Calculate work done by a force and use W = Fs cos θ.
Use KE = ½mv² and GPE = mgh.
Define power as rate of work done: P = W/t = Fv.

simpleExplanation.title: Energy swaps, work transfers
simpleExplanation.summary: Work done transfers energy. On frictionless tracks, kinetic and potential energy interchange but total mechanical energy stays constant.
steps: Work = force × displacement in direction of force.
Energy conservation: initial KE + GPE = final KE + GPE (no friction).
Power measures how fast work is done — same work, less time → more power.

sections.content: **Energy methods** often provide faster solutions than kinematics on P4 — look for **conservation** opportunities before writing SUVAT equations.

sections.question: A 2 kg object falls from rest through 5 m. Find speed just before impact (g = 9.8).
